name_full: First Aid to Cultural Heritage in Times of Crisis - Handbook
issuer: International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property
sector_applicability: Museums archives libraries heritage custodians emergency managers humanitarian responders civil protection agencies and communities
why_it_matters: Provides practical workflows and tools for securing tangible and intangible cultural heritage during disasters conflict and complex humanitarian crises
key_outputs: Emergency first aid method and tools for cultural heritage
notes: ICCROM page identifies the handbook as issued in 2018 and aimed at cultural heritage and emergency responders

name_full: ICOM Code of Ethics for Museums
standard_id: ICOM Code of Ethics for Museums
sector_applicability: Museums museum professionals cultural property custodians collection managers and heritage governance bodies
why_it_matters: Sets minimum professional standards for museum practice including acquisitions provenance due diligence restitution security and compliance with legislation
key_outputs: Ethical principles and professional practice guidelines for museums
data_source: ICOM official standards and guidelines page
notes: ICOM states the Code was first adopted in 1986 and revised in 2004 while a new revision process is underway

issuer: International Council on Monuments and Sites
sector_applicability: World Heritage evaluators conservation professionals heritage managers cultural ministries and cultural landscape specialists
why_it_matters: Expands authenticity assessment beyond a single material model so heritage values can be evaluated in cultural context
key_outputs: Authenticity principles for heritage conservation and World Heritage practice
data_source: ICOMOS International Scientific Committee on Cultural Landscapes official page
notes: ICOMOS page describes the Nara Document as drafted at the 1994 Nara Conference with UNESCO ICCROM and ICOMOS cooperation

name_full: International Charter for the Conservation and Restoration of Monuments and Sites
issuer: International Council on Monuments and Sites
sector_applicability: Conservation architects heritage authorities monument custodians restoration specialists and World Heritage practitioners
why_it_matters: Establishes foundational international principles for conserving and restoring monuments and sites while preserving authenticity
key_outputs: International conservation and restoration charter for monuments and sites
notes: ICOMOS identifies the Venice Charter as adopted by ICOMOS in 1965 after the 1964 Venice congress

name_full: Convention on the Protection and Promotion of the Diversity of Cultural Expressions
issuer: United Nations Educational Scientific and Cultural Organization
sector_applicability: States cultural ministries creative industries cultural policy bodies artists communities and development partners
why_it_matters: Provides a binding international framework for protecting and promoting diverse cultural expressions and integrating culture into sustainable development
key_outputs: Convention text implementation reporting and cooperation framework
data_source: UNESCO Legal Affairs official instrument page
notes: UNESCO Legal Affairs page gives adoption on 20 October 2005 and entry into force on 18 March 2007

name_full: Convention for the Protection of Cultural Property in the Event of Armed Conflict
issuer: United Nations Educational Scientific and Cultural Organization
sector_applicability: States armed forces civil protection cultural heritage authorities museums archives and humanitarian actors
why_it_matters: Creates the core treaty framework for safeguarding and respecting cultural property during armed conflict
key_outputs: Convention text regulations and protocols for cultural property protection
data_source: UNESCO Legal Affairs official instrument page
notes: UNESCO Legal Affairs page gives adoption at The Hague on 14 May 1954 and entry into force on 7 August 1956

name_full: Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage
issuer: United Nations Educational Scientific and Cultural Organization
sector_applicability: States communities culture ministries indigenous peoples heritage practitioners and safeguarding programmes
why_it_matters: Establishes the global treaty framework for identifying safeguarding and transmitting living heritage practices knowledge and expressions
key_outputs: Convention text lists assistance mechanisms reporting and safeguarding obligations
data_source: UNESCO Legal Affairs official instrument page
notes: UNESCO Legal Affairs page gives adoption on 17 October 2003 and entry into force on 20 April 2006

name_full: Convention Concerning the Protection of the World Cultural and Natural Heritage
issuer: United Nations Educational Scientific and Cultural Organization
sector_applicability: States heritage site managers conservation authorities environmental agencies indigenous peoples tourism planners and communities
why_it_matters: Creates the global framework for identifying protecting conserving and transmitting cultural and natural heritage of outstanding universal value
key_outputs: Convention text World Heritage List governance and international assistance framework
data_source: UNESCO World Heritage Centre official convention text
notes: World Heritage Centre states the General Conference adopted the Convention on 16 November 1972
